questions.question: What is the value of boolean b after: boolean b = true || false && false;
questions.explanation: && has higher precedence than ||, so expression is true || (false && false) => true || false => true.

questions.question: Given: int x = 5; String s; if (x > 5) { s = "A"; } else if (x == 5) { s = "B"; } else { s = "C"; } What is s?
questions.explanation: x equals 5, so the else-if branch runs and assigns "B".

questions.question: How many times does count increment? int count = 0; for (int i = 1; i <= 3; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 2; j++) { count++; } }
questions.explanation: Outer loop runs 3 times; inner runs 2 times each: 3*2 = 6 increments.

questions.question: What is printed? int n = 2; String s = ""; switch (n) { case 1: s += "a"; case 2: s += "b"; break; case 3: s += "c"; } System.out.print(s);
questions.explanation: Execution jumps to case 2 and appends "b" then breaks. No fall-through from case 1 occurs.

questions.question: Which loop guarantees the body executes at least once?
questions.explanation: do-while evaluates its condition after the first iteration.

questions.question: What happens? int x = 0; if (x != 0 && 10 / x > 1) { System.out.println("yes"); }
options: C) No output; no exception due to short-circuit
questions.explanation: x != 0 is false; with && the RHS is not evaluated, so no exception and nothing prints.

questions.question: How many numbers print? for (int i = 0; i <= 5; i++) { System.out.print(i + " "); }
questions.explanation: i takes values 0 through 5 inclusive, which is 6 numbers.

questions.question: Using -1 as a sentinel, what sum is computed? int sum = 0; int val; // Input: 3 4 -1 5 while ((val = sc.nextInt()) != -1) { sum += val; } System.out.print(sum);
questions.explanation: Only 3 and 4 are added before encountering -1. The 5 after -1 is not read.

questions.question: Which condition avoids NullPointerException if s may be null when checking for "yes"?
questions.explanation: "yes".equals(s) is safe when s is null; calling s.equals would throw a NullPointerException.

questions.question: What is printed? for (int i = 1; i <= 4; i++) { if (i % 2 == 0) continue; System.out.print(i); }
questions.explanation: continue skips even i values, so prints 1 and 3.
